Translation (#4)

* Add translation module with MiniMessage support

Introduce a new "translation" Gradle module and include it in settings.gradle. The module provides a MiniMessage-based translation system integrated with Adventure: public APIs (TranslationManager, MiniMessageTranslator, MiniMessageTranslationRegistry, TranslationSource, TranslationMigrator), configuration (FileSourceOptions + impl), implementations for registry and translator, file factory for YAML/properties files with creation, migration and backup logic, and utilities (LocalizedException, MiniMessages, tags). Also add comprehensive unit tests and sample translation resources (en_US, de_DE, ja_JP, zh_CN). This enables loading, migrating, and rendering locale-specific MiniMessage translations and registering them with Adventure.

* Add JDK locale fallback and translation cache

Introduce a LocaleFallback API and a JdkLocaleFallback implementation (singleton) that computes CLDR-style candidate chains, provides parent lookup, supports a configurable root locale, and caches computed chains/parents. Update MiniMessageTranslationRegistryImpl to accept a LocaleFallback (defaulting to JdkLocaleFallback), add per-translation resolvedCache and cache invalidation on register, and resolve translations by iterating the fallback chain. Add unit tests for JdkLocaleFallback and several translation loading/performance benchmark tests. Also fix a missing static import in a GUI test.

* Refactor translation loader and parsers

Replace the old file-based factory and concrete source classes with a focused loader and parser layer. Removed FileSourceFactory, YamlTranslationSource and PropertiesTranslationSource; added TranslationLoader (handles creation, version checks, backups and migration/merge logic), TranslationParsers (YAML/Properties parsing and YAML flattening using SnakeYAML) and MapTranslationSource (map-backed TranslationSource). Updated TranslationManagerImpl to use TranslationLoader and adjusted tests to exercise the new parsers and MapTranslationSource. Migration/version handling, backup and file write logic were consolidated into TranslationLoader; parsers expose flattened key/value maps for use by the manager.

* Refactor tests and tidy translation APIs

Add test lifecycle logging/cleanup and reorganize test helper types, plus small API and implementation cleanups. Tests: add @AfterAll cleanup/summary methods and improved console output, move nested test config/part classes, clear TypeInference caches in setup/teardown, and adjust spacing/formatting across EnhancedConfigTest, TypeInferenceBenchmark and TypeInferenceTest. Translation API/impl: swap and clarify LocaleFallback root getter/setter, update JdkLocaleFallback accordingly, route TranslationManager.instance() through TranslationManagerImpl.getInstance(), adjust MiniMessage registry/translator/impl field ordering and javadoc param alignment, and other minor whitespace/formatting improvements. Mostly non-functional refactors and clearer test diagnostics.

* Refactor configuration manager into services

Large refactor that splits the monolithic ConfigurationManager into smaller services and registries. Introduces ConfigurationLoader, SerializationService, DeserializationService, ConfigurationSynchronizer, VersionManager and AdapterRegistry; ConfigurationManager now delegates to these services and exposes new TypeToken-based serializer APIs (registerTypeSerializer/deserialize). Adds a rich SerializationException for better error context and a FieldMeta record, moves several classes into service/model/integration packages, and centralizes logging via ConfigurationLogger. Also updates backup handling (ConfigBackupManager) to use the new logger and removes many inline I/O/serialization helpers from ConfigurationManager in favor of the new components.

* Move subprojects into modules/ and add i18n tests

Reorganize project layout by moving the configuration, gui and translation subprojects into a new modules/ directory while preserving their internal package structure. Add test translation resources (en_US.yml, de_DE.yml, ja_JP.properties, zh_CN.yml) under modules/translation/src/test/resources/translations and update settings.gradle to reflect the new paths. This cleans up the repository structure and adds sample i18n fixtures for translation tests.

* Add command module (GloomCommand framework)

Introduce a new modules/command module implementing the GloomCommand command framework for Paper/Brigadier. Adds core entrypoint GloomCommand (builder, lifecycle integration, DI, default argument resolvers), a CommandRegistry/ProcessorPipeline/MethodInvoker flow, many command-related annotations (Arg, Command, SubCommand, Usage, Permission, etc.), argument resolvers/suggestions, a fluent CommandBuilder, exception handling and processors. Also adds JMH benchmarks (CommandBenchmark, DispatchBenchmark), a basic example test, and a build.gradle configured for Java 21, JMH and Paper/Adventure compileOnly deps. This commit lays the foundation for declarative and programmatic command registration and execution with documentation/comments and example usage.

* Hybrid VarHandle/MethodHandle FieldMeta + tests

Replace FieldMeta record with a full class that chooses VarHandle for primitives and MethodHandle for object fields, adds annotation caching, compareAndSet for primitives, and wraps reflective access with clearer error handling. Remove a couple of redundant @SuppressWarnings cleanup in ConfigurationManager and SerializationService. Update .gitignore to include /docs and JMH paths. Add comprehensive performance and correctness tests (FieldMetaVarHandleTest, ReflectionPerformanceTest, ReflectionBenchmarkComparison, RealWorldConfigPerformanceTest) to validate the new hybrid strategy and measure reflection/annotation access performance.

* Refactor command module and optimize invocation

Introduce modular command components and performance improvements across the command module.

- Add BrigadierTreeBuilder, ArgumentParser and CommandExecutor to separate tree construction, argument resolution and execution concerns; add FastPathExecutor and several utility classes (CommandMessages, MessageUtils, ParameterUtils, TypeConverterUtil) to support these flows.
- Refactor CommandRegistry to coordinate new components, register fast-path commands, cache metadata (invokers, cooldown keys, async), and delegate parsing/execution to the new classes.
- Optimize MethodInvoker by adding a spread handle and parameter-count specialization (direct invokes for 0–8 args, fall back to spread/invokeWithArguments) to reduce reflection overhead.
- Update JMH configuration and benchmarks (longer warmup/measurement, configurable via properties, output files) and adjust CommandBenchmark/DispatchBenchmark to better exercise direct/reflection/invoker and dispatch paths.
- Minor changes: MessageService now uses MessageUtils.MINI_MESSAGE, improved error/cooldown/async handling, and add common gitignore entries.

* Translate command module docs to English

Convert Chinese comments and Javadocs to English across the command module and perform small related adjustments. Fix a typo (glooom -> gloom), add a new CommandMessages class, remove the old util/CommandMessages, and delete FastPathExecutor. Minor behavioral tweaks: default Cooldown.message set to empty, BrigadierTreeBuilder no longer imports CommandMessages (throws formatted literal), and various small clarifications/refactors in lifecycle/registration and annotation docs.

* Create temp_en_us.json

* Refactor command core, add unregister tracking

Major refactor of the command module: moved many components into a new core package, removed JMH benchmark classes, and introduced runtime command tracking and unregister support.

Key changes:
- Added internal classes: CommandMetadata, CommandTracker, CommandUnregistrar and a Reflection util.
- GloomCommand: added builder(), lifecycle wiring to initialize CommandUnregistrar, track registered names and store Paper Commands registrar; added unregisterCommand(by name and by instance), isCommandRegistered(), and getRegisteredCommandNames().
- CommandRegistry: moved to core package, accepts PluginMeta for registration, returns actual registered names (handles namespacing/conflicts), scans/warmups methods, builds brigadier tree, logs conflicts, and exposes constructors supporting a custom Logger.
- Moved/renamed parser/builder/executor packages into core and adjusted imports accordingly; reorganized several classes (ArgumentParser, BrigadierTreeBuilder, CommandBuilder, CommandExecutor) to reflect the package moves and small API tweaks.
- Small API/cleanup changes across annotations and context classes (javadoc formatting cleanup, removed duplicated AsyncContext.from, added AsyncContext.from overload), CommandResult/CommandCondition/CommandException minor fixes.
- Added a test for command unregistration (CommandUnregisterTest) and other minor improvements (suggestion provider imports, resolver cache clearing on unregister).

These changes enable safe per-instance command isolation, ownership-verified unregistration, improved registration conflict handling, and preparation/warmup of invokers for better runtime behavior.

* Delete FieldMeta.java

* Extract NamingUtils and refactor backup/util usage

Introduce NamingUtils.camelToKebab and centralize name conversions across serialization/deserialization/versioning/spark integration. Rename/move ConfigBackupManager -> gloomlib.configuration.util.ConfigBackup and update all call sites and tests. Replace direct stack traces with ConfigurationLogger calls and add a reusable ERROR_FILE_NOT_SET message in ConfigurationFile. Add ReflectionUtils.wrapReflectionException and use it in FieldMeta. Break up large methods into smaller helpers (template processing in ConfigurationLoader, record component handling in DeserializationService, validation checks in ConfigurationSynchronizer) and apply minor cleanup/formatting in tests and TypeInference. Overall: consolidation of naming/backup utilities, improved logging and error handling, and small refactors for readability and reuse.

* Refactor GUI click handling and navigation

Centralize and simplify GUI click logic: introduce ClickResult record, move click processing into processClick/applyClickChanges/notifyComponentClick, and streamline handlers in GloomGui. Rework ClickActionHandler with bundle helper methods, hotbar constant, prioritized shift-click logic (buildPrioritizedSlots/tryStackIntoPrioritySlots/tryPlaceInEmptyPrioritySlot) and reuse handleShiftClickWithPriority. Improve NavigationHistory by adding constants, snapshot-based deduplication of repeating sequences, and helper methods for clearer logic and safer bounds. Add constants and explicit window/client state tracking to AbstractWindow (row/size limits, client sync interval, timestamps). Misc: rename ignored exception vars in GloomGuiManager/BundleUtils, small javadoc/whitespace cleanups in GuiItemUtils, and remove an unnecessary @SuppressWarnings in CommandBuilder.

* Refactor command module: resolvers, messages, logging

Large refactor of the command module to centralize resolvers, modernize messaging and replace ad-hoc logging.

Key changes:
- Centralized built-in resolvers behind BuiltInResolvers.registerAll and added a resolver/registry package (many specific resolver files removed/relocated).
- Renamed/moved SuggestionProvider to gloomlib.command.core and updated usages; added richer javadoc and usage examples.
- Renamed TypeConverterUtil → TypeConverterUtils and updated call sites.
- Replaced System.err/printStackTrace usages with Adventure's ComponentLogger for contextual debug logging across BrigadierTreeBuilder, CommandExecutor, BrigadierUnregister, ExceptionResolverRegistry, ProcessorPipeline, etc.
- Overhauled CommandMessages to use MiniMessage-based fallbacks, simplified API (get() / get(Object...)), and added structured message keys for the interactive help system; added messages.yml resource.
- Updated CommandHelp to produce interactive Adventure components (click/hover, pagination), and wired MessageUtils for MiniMessage rendering.
- Minor API/implementation cleanups and documentation improvements (MethodInvoker/CommandMetadata/CommandTracker etc.), plus added tests/examples for resolver discovery and help features.

These changes aim to improve extensibility (resolver registry), UX (interactive help + MiniMessage), and maintainability (consistent logging and clearer APIs).

* Fix Javadoc issues and remove command Gradle file

Add build_log.txt with javadoc failure output; update gradle/libs.versions.toml (paper version adjusted); remove modules/command/build.gradle. Fix numerous Javadoc and API documentation problems across the command module (add {@code} wrappers, close HTML tags, add @param/@return descriptions for records/methods, and small formatting/whitespace tweaks) to address Javadoc generation errors and improve doc accuracy.

* Fix multiple bugs flagged in PR review: enum resolver, Range defaults, charset, arg substitution, alias tracking, and more (#5)

* Initial plan

* Apply review feedback: fix Range, enums, charset, arg order, translate delegate, tracker, analyzer, NavigationHistory, benchmark

Add production-grade sections to file storage design

New sections:
- 7k. Folder move/rename transactions: pointer-based tree with O(1)
  parent_id update, lazy path cache invalidation, cycle detection

  that overrides GC, compliance admin audit trail

  virtual filesystem drivers (macOS File Provider, Windows Cloud
  Files API), placeholder files, eviction policy, pinning

- Link revocation propagation: CDN purge + API Gateway deny-list +
  short-TTL signed URLs as defense-in-depth

fix: always repaint Live View so it mirrors every parent action

Previously, child repaints were only sent when sync_live_view_shared
detected a data change (current_index, chunk_size, theme, etc.).
Actions like play/pause that don't immediately change those fields
left the Live View stale until the next stream tick or mouse hover.

Now the parent unconditionally requests a child repaint every frame
while the Live View is open, gated only by the stale-child guard.
egui caches layout internally so redundant repaints are cheap.

fix: use dlopen for EGL lookup; restore full background rendering

The previous dlsym(RTLD_DEFAULT) approach silently failed because glutin
loads libEGL.so with RTLD_LOCAL, hiding its symbols from the global
table.  Now we dlopen("libEGL.so.1", RTLD_LAZY) to get an explicit
handle, then dlsym on that handle — this correctly finds and calls
eglSwapInterval(0), making swap_buffers non-blocking.

With swap_interval(0) actually working:
- Removed the focus gate from the parent: unfocused-but-visible children
  keep receiving repaints and track the word stream in real time.
- Removed the child early-exit on unfocused: background-visible Live
  View renders full content, not an empty panel.
- Stale-child guard (1 s) remains as a safety net for compositors that
  don't honor swap interval 0.

commit.message:
Fix connparse handling for scheme-less `//...` WSH shorthand URIs (#3006)

`pkg/remote/connparse` was failing on shorthand WSH inputs that omit the
`wsh://` scheme, including remote hosts, WSL targets, and Windows local
paths. The parser was splitting on `://` too early and misclassifying
leading `//` inputs before WSH shorthand handling ran.

- **What changed**
- Detect scheme-less WSH shorthand up front with `strings.HasPrefix(uri,
"//")`
- Route those inputs through the existing WSH path parsing flow instead
of the generic `://` split path
- Reuse the same shorthand flag when deciding whether to parse as
remote/local WSH vs current-path shorthand

- **Behavioral impact**
- `//conn/path/to/file` now parses as host `conn` with path
`path/to/file`
- `//wsl://Ubuntu/path/to/file` now preserves the WSL host and absolute
path shape
- `//local/C:\path\to\file` now parses as local Windows shorthand
instead of being treated as a current-path string

- **Scope**
  - Keeps the existing test expectations intact
  - Limits the change to `pkg/remote/connparse/connparse.go`

```go
isWshShorthand := strings.HasPrefix(uri, "//")

if isWshShorthand {
    rest = strings.TrimPrefix(uri, "//")
} else if len(split) > 1 {
    scheme = split[0]
    rest = strings.TrimPrefix(split[1], "//")
}
```

commit.message:
[ts-http-runtime] Fix URL construction in urlHelpers.ts (#37302)

There are cases where the `routePath` isn't a real path, but in the form
of `"?restype=service&comp=properties"`. They are supposed to be query
parameters but today are appended to the endpoint directly. This caused
issues when the endpoint already contains query parameters (e.g.,
storage SAS URL).

This PR handles this case and ports code from `@azure/core-client` for appending path and merging query params.


### Packages impacted by this PR
`@typespec/ts-http-runtime`

commit.message:
Add shift ops, fix macros, improve _byte_ge_const_flag n=128

Macro system:
- Fix $param substitution in parameterized macros: ADD(x,y) with
  'set $a + $b on a' now correctly expands to 'set $x + $y on x'
- Add #ifdef / #ifndef / #else / #endif conditional compilation
- Add #if 1 / #if 0 for simple conditional blocks
- Add #elif for chained conditionals
- Add backslash line continuation for multi-line macro definitions
- Stack-based tracking ensures nested conditionals work correctly

Shift operators (<< and >>):
- Tokenizer now recognizes '<<' and '>>' as two-character tokens
- 'set $x << N on y' and 'set $x >> N on y' compile for byte/int types
- Right operand must be a compile-time integer constant
- Emits N successive calls to _shift_left/right_multi_byte

_byte_ge_const_flag n=128 performance:
- Add _extract_bit7(pos, out_flag): uses 7 applications of _divmod2_cell
  to extract MSB in O(255) BF steps vs O(16384) for _compare_bytes_unsigned
- _byte_ge_const_flag(pos, 128, flag) now routes through _extract_bit7
- This speeds up _shift_left_multi_byte at BF runtime (64x for n=128 case)
  making left-shifts and multiplication much faster when executed

Tests:
- New tests/test_macros.py: 37 tests covering tokenizer, conditionals,
  line continuation, dollar-param macros, shift operators, and integration
- Total test count: 111 passing

commit.message:
fix: gate repaints on child focus to prevent Wayland minimize freeze

Three layers of defense against the minimize freeze:

1. eglSwapInterval(0) — makes swap_buffers non-blocking (primary)
2. Parent focus gate — skips request_repaint_of when child_focused is
   Some(false), preventing new repaints from being queued
3. Child early-exit — when unfocused, renders an empty panel and
   returns immediately, catching stray repaints already in the queue

When the Live View is unfocused but visible (user watching the main
stream), OS events (mouse hover/enter) still trigger child renders
that pick up the latest shared state on demand.
